tips.title: Route by difficulty, not by habit
tips.body: Default to Sonnet for edits, tests, and refactors; escalate to Opus only for hard reasoning or ambiguous design work. Most teams cut spend 30-50% with no quality loss.

tips.title: Prompt caching is your biggest lever
tips.body: Cached input reads cost ~10x less than fresh input. Keep your file set and early messages stable within a session so the cache stays warm.

tips.title: Haiku 4.5 is great for boilerplate
tips.body: For mechanical tasks — renames, formatting, simple scaffolding — Haiku is ~18x cheaper than Opus on output. Consider it your default for low-stakes turns.
